markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games has locked Grand Theft Auto VI for a November 19, 2026 release on PS5 and Xbox Series X/S following two prior delays, with Take-Two Interactive embedding the date in its latest earnings guidance and projecting strong fiscal 2027 bookings tied to the launch. This firm timeline, just five months out as of mid-June 2026, has shifted trader focus toward imminent marketing catalysts such as a potential third trailer, the final major GTA Online update in July, and any earnings-call commentary on polish or pre-order tracking. Industry-wide effects are already visible, with competing fall 2026 titles adjusting schedules to avoid direct overlap in a market dominated by GTA VI's cultural and commercial weight. High anticipation for Vice City-set gameplay details and streaming metrics keeps sentiment anchored to official Rockstar channels rather than unverified rumors, while the compressed window before launch heightens sensitivity to any last-minute status updates.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: **High trader consensus on "No Prison Time" (80.8%)** reflects Weinstein's advanced age (74), documented health decline including cancer and diabetes, and a string of mistrials plus hung juries in his New York retrials through mid-2026. The 2025 split verdict and May 2026 mistrial on the remaining rape charge have left additional New York exposure limited, while defense efforts focus on concurrent sentencing or plea resolutions that add little beyond time already served. His separate 16-year California term runs consecutively but starts later, and traders appear to price in realistic prospects of release or minimal further incarceration given his condition and ongoing appeals. Lower-probability buckets for multi-year sentences capture residual risks from any successful prosecution on unresolved counts, yet recent courtroom dynamics have kept those outcomes sidelined.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: Carolina Hurricanes hold an 79.5% implied probability as 2026 Stanley Cup champions due to their 3-2 series lead over the Vegas Golden Knights following a 4-2 home victory in Game 5. The Hurricanes have dominated earlier playoff rounds, advancing past the Senators, Flyers, and Canadiens with strong defensive structure and special-teams efficiency, while posting superior regular-season records. Vegas reached the Final after eliminating the Avalanche in four games but trails after high-scoring contests that featured multiple lead changes and overtime thrillers. Game 6 shifts to T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas on June 14, where the Golden Knights seek to force a decisive Game 7, though Carolina's momentum and home-ice advantage earlier in the series underpin trader consensus on the current outcome probabilities.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France sit at the head of the 2026 World Cup winner market because both squads combine recent major-tournament success with exceptional depth and star power. Spain’s Euro 2024 triumph, Nations League title, and Olympic gold have reinforced a possession-dominant style built around Lamine Yamal and a balanced midfield, while France’s back-to-back final appearances and consistent FIFA ranking near the top reflect Didier Deschamps’ defensive organization and attacking options led by Kylian Mbappé. England’s perfect qualifying record under Thomas Tuchel, Portugal’s pursuit of a first title with Cristiano Ronaldo, and Argentina’s status as defending champions further tighten the race, with Brazil and several other European sides maintaining realistic paths through strong recent form and favorable early bracket positioning. The expanded 48-team field and clustered elite talent keep implied probabilities bunched among the leading contenders.

England’s perfect qualifying record under Thomas Tuchel, Portugal’s pursuit of a first title with Cristiano Ronaldo, and Argentina’s status as defending champions further tighten the race, with Brazil and several other European sides maintaining realistic paths through strong recent form and favorable early bracket positioning. The expanded 48-team field and clustered elite talent keep implied probabilities bunched among the leading contenders.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Term limits were eliminated in 2018, enabling indefinite tenure, and the 20th Party Congress in 2022 delivered his third term with no designated successor. Preparations for a fourth term at the 2027 congress are widely anticipated, as senior roles remain filled by his allies and no factional challenges have surfaced. Recent diplomacy, including the May 2026 Trump summit in Beijing and the June visit to North Korea, further signals continuity in leadership. Ongoing personnel purges have reinforced rather than eroded his authority, with no verifiable developments indicating an early exit.
